methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Quilter is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Sergiy Nesterenko that has raised $15M+ to date. The valuation analysis relies on a peer universe of six private companies in the same sector and stage band (Robotics & Manufacturing, Series B and adjacent stages), with a broader comp set including one public company and eight additional private peers such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis uses the $15M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to next-stage advancement, implying a $30M valuation. Peer-median analysis draws on six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.1B, though with material dispersion: the range spans $300M (Fairmat, Collaborative Robotics) to $10.0B (1X Technologies).

These two methods produce a triangulated range of $26M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $552M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, reflecting early-stage risk and capital deployed. The peer median dominates the upper half, but the wide peer range—driven by heterogeneity in 1X Technologies' outlier $10B valuation—signals substantial uncertainty. Key unknowns include Quilter's differentiation within the competitive robotics stack, path to revenue, and whether it tracks toward the $300M–$1.8B band of nearer-stage comparables or toward tier-one outliers.
